metadata.description: Azure region for all resources.

Globally-unique suffix appended to resources that require unique names (ACR, Content Safety, APIM, etc.). Default: a deterministic 6-char hash of your subscription id, guaranteeing uniqueness across customers. Override with your own short alphanumeric token.
Tags applied to every resource.
Log Analytics workspace name.
Application Insights name.
Content Safety account name (must be globally unique).
Public IP name (regional uniqueness only).
NAT Gateway name.
Main NSG name.
GPU subnet NSG name.
Virtual network name.
Azure Container Registry name (lowercase alphanumeric only, globally unique).
Container Apps Environment name.
Main application Container App name.
Stage-2 GPU Container App name.
API Management service name (globally unique).
Premium FileStorage account name for NFS hfcache share (globally unique, 3-24 lowercase alphanumeric).
NFS file share name on the storage account.
Quota for the NFS share, GiB.
VNet address space.
Subnet for Container Apps Environment (workload profile, non-GPU).
Subnet reserved for GPU workloads (Microsoft.Storage service endpoint).
Subnet for APIM v2 (delegated to Microsoft.Web/serverFarms).
Content Safety SKU.
ACR SKU. Premium needed for zone-redundancy and private endpoints.
Image for ca-ratio-contentshield. Defaults to mcr quickstart so the template can deploy before customer images are pushed. Overridden by appImageTag when that is non-empty.
Image for ca-contentshield-stage2 (GPU). Defaults to mcr quickstart. Overridden by stage2ImageTag when that is non-empty.

Tag for ca-ratio-contentshield in the customer ACR. When non-empty, builds the full image reference as "<acrLoginServer>/contentshield:<tag>" and overrides appImage. Leave empty to use appImage as-is (placeholder/dev).

Tag for ca-contentshield-stage2 in the customer ACR. When non-empty, builds the full image reference as "<acrLoginServer>/contentshield-stage2:<tag>" and overrides stage2Image. Leave empty to use stage2Image as-is (placeholder/dev).
Target port for ca-ratio-contentshield.
Target port for ca-contentshield-stage2.
GPU workload profile name. Default NC24-A100. Set to empty string to skip the GPU profile (and skip stage2 GPU deployment).
GPU workload profile type. Common: Consumption-GPU-NC24-A100, Consumption-GPU-NC8as-T4.
Deploy the stage-2 GPU container app. Set false to skip (e.g., during initial testing if GPU quota unavailable).
Extra IP CIDR ranges to add to stage-2 ingress allowlist (e.g., the stage-2 app outbound IP after first deploy).
Optional Hugging Face token for stage-2 model download. Leave empty to skip the secret.
Optional NFS Azure Files server (e.g., mystorage.file.core.windows.net) used to mount HF cache for stage-2. Leave empty to skip storage mount.
Optional NFS share path (e.g., /mystorage/hfcache). Leave empty to skip storage mount.
Model name advertised to the main app (SLM_MODEL) and served by stage-2 (MODEL_NAME).
Feature flag CONTENTSHIELD_V1_ML_DISABLED on the main app. "1" disables the legacy ML path; "0" re-enables it.
Deploy a brand-new Premium FileStorage account + NFS hfcache share inside this RG. When true, the values of nfsServer/nfsShareName are ignored and computed from the new storage account.

Deploy the HuggingFace cache pre-warm job. The job runs on demand (kicked off by deploy.ps1) and populates the NFS hfcache share with the stage-2 SLM weights so the first GPU cold start skips the multi-minute download. Requires deployStorage=true and deployStage2=true to be useful.
Container Apps Job name for the HF cache pre-warm.
APIM SKU. StandardV2 supports VNet integration in West US 3.
APIM capacity (units).
APIM publisher email.
APIM publisher name.
Deploy API Management. Slow (~30-45 min). Set false to skip during iterative testing.
AAD tenant id stamped into APIM named values (used by validate-azure-ad-token policy). Defaults to the tenant the deployment is running in.
AAD audience that the APIM policy validates incoming bearer tokens against. Defaults to ratioAiDevClientId — set to the API app registration client id for production.
AAD client (application) id of the API. Stored as an APIM named value. Defaults to ratioAiDevClientId.
Client (Application) ID of the Entra app registration RatioAIDev. Exposed to container apps as RATIO_AI_DEV_CLIENT_ID.
Workspace retention in days (Pay-as-you-go default 30).

properties.description: Created by Azure Core Security managed policy, placeholder you can delete
Created by Azure Core Security managed policy
Created by Azure Core Security managed policy
DO NOT DELETE - Azure Core Security
DO NOT DELETE - Azure Core Security
DO NOT DELETE - Azure Core Security
DO NOT DELETE - Azure Core Security
DO NOT DELETE - Azure Core Security

metadata.description: Globally-unique storage account name (3-24, lowercase alphanumeric).
File share quota in GiB (Premium file shares: min 100).
Subnet resource IDs that are allowed to reach the storage account (Microsoft.Storage service endpoint must be enabled on each).
NFS share name.
Root-squash policy on the NFS share.
GPU workload profile name. Empty string skips the GPU profile.
GPU workload profile type. e.g. Consumption-GPU-NC24-A100.
NFS Azure Files server (empty to skip).
NFS share name (empty to skip).
Content Safety account resource id — used to grant the main app MI Cognitive Services User on that account.
Static IP of the Container Apps Environment (used as a stage-2 ingress allowlist entry so the app can reach itself / sibling apps via internal ingress).
Optional list of extra IP CIDR ranges to allow on stage-2 ingress.
Model name the SLM serves — exposed to the main app via SLM_MODEL.
Feature flag CONTENTSHIELD_V1_ML_DISABLED on the main app. Set to "0" to re-enable the legacy ML path.
Minimum replicas for the main app. Keep ≥1 to avoid scale-to-zero cold starts.
Minimum replicas for the stage-2 GPU app. Keep ≥1 to keep a GPU node warm and avoid 5-15 min cold starts on a new replica.
Maximum replicas for the stage-2 GPU app.
Storage account name. Used to compute the mount path /mount/<accountName>/<share>.
NFS share name used as the mount sub-path (also the volume name in CAE storage).

variables.stage2EnvBase: [concat(createArray(createObject('name', 'MODEL_NAME', 'value', parameters('slmModel')), createObject('name', 'MAX_MODEL_LEN', 'value', '20000'), createObject('name', 'GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION', 'value', '0.9'), createObject('name', 'LANGUAGE_MODEL_ONLY', 'value', 'true'), createObject('name', 'EXTRA_ENGINE_ARGS', 'value', '--enable-prefix-caching'), createObject('name', 'HF_HOME', 'value', variables('hfCacheMountPath')), createObject('name', 'ENABLE_THINKING', 'value', 'false'), createObject('name', 'CACHE_ENABLED', 'value', 'true'), createObject('name', 'APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING', 'value', parameters('appInsightsConnectionString'))), createArray())]
variables.stage2CaeRule: [if(empty(parameters('caeStaticIp')), createArray(), createArray(createObject('name', 'AllowACAEnvStatic', 'description', 'Allow same ACA environment static IP', 'action', 'Allow', 'ipAddressRange', format('{0}/32', parameters('caeStaticIp')))))]

containers.name: [parameters('appName')]
containers.env: [concat(createArray(createObject('name', 'CONTENT_SAFETY_ENDPOINT', 'value', parameters('contentSafetyEndpoint')), createObject('name', 'CONTENT_SAFETY_API_VERSION', 'value', '2024-09-01'), createObject('name', 'SLM_ENDPOINT', 'value', format('http://{0}', parameters('stage2AppName'))), createObject('name', 'SLM_PATH', 'value', '/classify'), createObject('name', 'SLM_MODEL', 'value', parameters('slmModel')), createObject('name', 'SLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS', 'value', '60'), createObject('name', 'CONTENTSHIELD_V1_ML_DISABLED', 'value', parameters('contentshieldV1MlDisabled')), createObject('name', 'APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING', 'value', parameters('appInsightsConnectionString')), createObject('name', 'RATIO_AI_DEV_CLIENT_ID', 'value', parameters('ratioAiDevClientId'))), createArray())]

metadata.description: Model id to pre-download into the HF cache (e.g. google/gemma-4-31b-it).
Storage account name. Used to compute the mount path /mount/<accountName>/<share> — must match what stage-2 uses.
NFS share name registered on the CAE as a storage definition (must already exist as a managedEnvironments/storages child).
CAE storage definition name to bind. Defaults to "hfcache" (the one defined in containerAppsEnv.bicep).
Optional HF token for gated/private models.
App Insights connection string — surfaced to the job for traceability.
Image used to run the download. MS-hosted to avoid Docker Hub rate limits.
Job replica timeout in seconds. Default 3600 (1h) — generous for cold cache + large models.

variables.downloadScript:
set -euo pipefail
echo "[prewarm] model=$MODEL_NAME"
echo "[prewarm] cache=$HF_HOME"
mkdir -p "$HF_HOME"
df -h "$HF_HOME" || true
echo "[prewarm] installing huggingface_hub + hf_transfer..."
python -m pip install --quiet --no-cache-dir --disable-pip-version-check \
    'huggingface_hub[cli]>=0.24' 'hf_transfer>=0.1.6'
echo "[prewarm] downloading..."
time huggingface-cli download "$MODEL_NAME" --cache-dir "$HF_HOME" --quiet
echo "[prewarm] done. footprint:"
du -sh "$HF_HOME" || true

metadata.description: CAE default domain (e.g. blacksea-a90e1d7c.westus3.azurecontainerapps.io) — used to compute the backend FQDN.
Container app name that serves the API (used to construct the backend URL https://<appName>.<caeDefaultDomain>).
AAD tenant id used by the validate-azure-ad-token policy.
AAD audience claim that the API validates incoming bearer tokens against. Usually the API app registration client id.
AAD client (application) id of the API. Stored as a named value for reference in policies.
Application Insights resource id to wire as an APIM logger. Empty string skips logger + diagnostics.
Application Insights instrumentation key (used as the logger credential).
Path the API is exposed under (gateway URL becomes https://<apim>.azure-api.net/<apiPath>).
API id (unique within APIM).
API display name shown in the developer portal.
Backend id (unique within APIM).

variables.apiPolicyXml:
[replace('<policies>
  <inbound>
    <base />
    <validate-azure-ad-token tenant-id="{{aad-tenant-id}}" header-name="Authorization" failed-validation-httpcode="401" failed-validation-error-message="Unauthorized">
      <audiences>
        <audience>{{aad-api-audience}}</audience>
      </audiences>
    </validate-azure-ad-token>
    <rate-limit calls="1000" renewal-period="60" remaining-calls-header-name="X-RateLimit-Remaining" total-calls-header-name="X-RateLimit-Limit" />
    <choose>
      <when condition="@(context.Request.Headers.ContainsKey("x-correlation-id"))">
        <set-header name="x-correlation-id" exists-action="override">
          <value>@(context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("x-correlation-id",""))</value>
        </set-header>
      </when>
      <otherwise>
        <set-header name="x-correlation-id" exists-action="override">
          <value>@(context.RequestId.ToString())</value>
        </set-header>
      </otherwise>
    </choose>
    <set-backend-service backend-id="__BACKEND_ID__" />
    <set-header name="Authorization" exists-action="delete" />
  </inbound>
  <backend>
    <forward-request timeout="30" />
  </backend>
  <outbound>
    <base />
  </outbound>
  <on-error>
    <base />
  </on-error>
</policies>', '__BACKEND_ID__', parameters('backendId'))]
